name: Bisket Jatra
description: Nepali New Year festival in Bhaktapur. The chariot of Bhairav is pulled through the streets in a tug-of-war between upper and lower Bhaktapur. Includes the Yosin (lingo) pole raising and tongue-piercing.
route_description: From Taumadhi Square through main streets to Khalna Tole, then the lingo pole raising at the river confluence

name: Rato Machindranath Jatra
description: Longest chariot festival in Nepal, lasting up to a month. The deity of rain (Machindranath) is pulled in a towering chariot through Patan's streets to ensure good monsoon rains.
route_description: Starts at Pulchowk, moves through Gabahal, Mangal Bazar, Sundhara, ending at Jawalakhel for Bhoto Jatra

name: Seto Machindranath Jatra
description: White Machindranath chariot festival in Kathmandu. Shorter than Patan's Rato version. The white-faced deity is paraded through Kathmandu's old neighborhoods.
route_description: From Jamal through Ason, Indra Chowk to Lagan

name: Gai Jatra
description: Festival of the Sacred Cow. Families who lost someone that year parade through town with a decorated cow (or a boy dressed as one). Features satirical performances mocking society and politics.
route_description: Procession through main streets and market squares

name: Indra Jatra
description: Eight-day festival honoring Indra. Features the Kumari chariot procession, display of Akash Bhairav mask, Pulu Kisi (elephant dance), and Lakhe (demon dance).
route_description: Kumari's chariot from Durbar Square through Ason, Indra Chowk, and back

name: Yenya Punhi
description: Ancient name for Indra Jatra in Nepal Bhasa. The festival includes erecting a ceremonial pole (lingo) at Hanuman Dhoka and displaying images of Bhairav dispensing rice beer.
route_description: Same route as Indra Jatra — centered on Kathmandu Durbar Square

name: Machindranath Snan
description: Bathing ceremony of the Rato Machindranath deity before the chariot festival begins. The wooden image is ceremonially bathed and repainted by Newar artisan priests.
route_description: At the Machindranath temple in Bungmati or Ta:bahal

name: Bhoto Jatra
description: Finale of the Rato Machindranath Jatra. The sacred jeweled vest (bhoto) is displayed to the crowd from the chariot at Jawalakhel. Crowds witness but no one may claim it.
route_description: Final stop of the Rato Machindranath chariot at Jawalakhel open field
